description: Warm seasonal greeting post celebrating Islamic or national holidays with blessings and well-wishes, typically featuring Arabic text with emoji accents and branded hashtags.
structural_recipe: Post a warm greeting message in Arabic wishing followers well during holidays (Eid, Ramadan, National Day, etc.). Structure: Opening greeting phrase + blessing/prayer line + relevant emoji (crescent moon for Islamic holidays) + brand hashtags. Keep tone warm, communal, and inclusive. Include branded or campaign-specific hashtags to amplify reach.
why_it_works: Holiday greetings tap into strong cultural and religious values in Saudi society. They demonstrate brand respect for shared traditions, build emotional connection through celebration of important moments, and position the brand as part of the community rather than external. This creates goodwill and strengthens customer loyalty during culturally significant periods.
transplantation_caveats: Holiday dates and significance vary by Islamic lunar calendar—timing must align with actual holiday dates to avoid appearing inauthentic
Greeting phrasing must be culturally appropriate and grammatically correct Arabic to resonate with local audience
Avoid any imagery or messaging that could conflict with Islamic or Saudi cultural values
Post timing should coincide with actual holiday period for maximum relevance and engagement
cultural_constraints: Must respect Islamic calendar and religious significance
Language must be formal, respectful Arabic
Imagery should align with Islamic aesthetics and values
Avoid religious content that may be divisive or inappropriate
